full_response:
First write the observer’s world-line in Minkowski (Cartesian) coordinates.  
For a constant proper acceleration α directed along the +x–axis the standard Rindler (hyperbolic) trajectory is

t(τ)   = 1 α sinh(ατ) ,    x(τ) = 1 α cosh(ατ),    y(τ)=0, z(τ)=0.     (1)

(The course notes set c = 1; if you prefer to keep c explicit, simply replace t → ct everywhere below.)

The 4-velocity is the derivative of the position four-vector x^μ(τ) = (t, x, y, z) with respect to the proper time τ:

U^μ ≡ dx^μ dτ .                                                     (2)

Differentiate (1):

U^0 = dt dτ = cosh(ατ),                                             (3a)
U^1 = dx dτ = sinh(ατ),                                             (3b)
U^2 = dy dτ = 0,                                                    (3c)
U^3 = dz dτ = 0.                                                    (3d)

Thus, in Cartesian Minkowski coordinates,

\boxed{\,U^\mu(\tau)=\bigl(\cosh(\alpha\tau),\;\sinh(\alpha\tau),\;0,\;0\bigr)\,.}

(If c is not set to 1, the temporal component is U^0 = c cosh(ατ).)

Confidence: 83%
